# Alpha Gate

> A self-hosted distribution gate for notarized macOS apps updated via Sparkle. It serves private,
> per-tester download links and Sparkle update feeds from the developer's own Cloudflare account
> (Workers + D1 + R2, free tier). Open source.

What it does: each tester gets one private link covering the download, activation, and their
personal update feed. Links are revocable and restorable. Publishing a notarized .dmg or .zip is
one command (`npx alpha-gate publish MyApp.dmg --channel beta`); setup is one command
(`npx alpha-gate deploy --instance myalpha`). A back office behind Cloudflare Access shows which
build every tester receives, with release channels, per-tester pins, and roll-forward rollback.

Key facts:
- The signing keys (Developer ID, notarization, Sparkle EdDSA) never leave the developer's Mac;
  the server stores archive bytes plus one signature string per archive and never signs.
- The update feed (Sparkle appcast) is generated per request, per tester, from a database.
- Sparkle cannot downgrade; rollback is a re-release with a higher build number.
- Tester tokens travel in URLs; unknown tokens get a plain 404; revoked testers get a
  renew-access notice instead of updates.
- Everything runs in the developer's own Cloudflare account: no vendor, nothing to subscribe to.

## Links

- [Website](https://alphagate.dev/)

- [Source code](https://github.com/alex-ivanov/alpha-gate)
- [Documentation index](https://github.com/alex-ivanov/alpha-gate/blob/main/docs/README.md)
- [Publishing guide](https://github.com/alex-ivanov/alpha-gate/blob/main/docs/operate/publish.md)
- [Architecture and invariants](https://github.com/alex-ivanov/alpha-gate/blob/main/docs/PRINCIPLES.md)
